finding: Caption claims V_eff(C) = (C^4/4) ln(C/Λ) − C^4/16 with critical point C₀ = Λe^(−1/4) ≈ 0.779Λ and depth −Λ⁴/(4e) ≈ −0.092. Caption-figure physics mismatch on the central object.
recommendation: Either rewrite caption to describe the G/G_c family OR regenerate figure to plot the closed-form V_eff with annotated C₀ and depth. Tier-1 standards prefer regeneration.

finding: Left-panel uses emerald-green (#2ca02c) 'locus' dashed line and burgundy-red (#a02050) 'M_Pl' dashed line per visualizations.py:10965-10974. Caption further uses 'green/yellow/red' verdict-band language, propagating the colorblind violation into the prose.
recommendation: Replace emerald 'locus' with steel-blue or plum; replace burgundy 'M_Pl' with amber or plum. Rewrite caption to drop 'green/yellow/red' nomenclature.

finding: Left-panel: four N_f curves (N_f ∈ {1,4,16,100}) overlap into a single visually-thick line — they cannot be distinguished by eye, defeating the parameter-scan purpose. recommendation: Differentiate N_f curves by linestyle (solid/dashed/dot/dashdot) in addition to color. Investigate / fix x-axis tick rendering artifact.

finding: Caption asserts 'Verdict bands: resolved (green), intermediate (yellow), reproduced (red)' as discrete bands. Right-panel rendered as a continuous dark-blue → grey → yellow Cividis-like colormap with NO discrete band shading; only a single yellow contour at log₁₀ ≈ 60 is visible.
recommendation: Add discrete verdict-band overlays to the right-panel heatmap if caption claims discrete bands, OR rewrite caption to describe the continuous log₁₀-ratio colormap with the single contour line.
